case_summary: Customer completed checkout and the payment went through, but no confirmation email arrived.
resolution_text: Agent verified the order and payment were successful, asked the customer to check spam and promotions folders, then re-sent the confirmation email successfully.
customer_message: I completed checkout and my card was charged, but I never got a confirmation email.

case_summary: Paid for the order successfully but never received the order confirmation message.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the transaction was captured, verified the email address on file, and manually re-triggered the confirmation email after asking the customer to check spam.
customer_message: I paid for my order but the confirmation email still hasn't shown up.

case_summary: Customer reports a successful card charge with no receipt or confirmation email after purchase.
resolution_text: Escalation reviewed payment logs, confirmed the order existed, and found a temporary notification delivery failure. The email was re-sent and delivered.

case_summary: Caller said payment completed but the confirmation email did not appear in the inbox.
resolution_text: Agent confirmed order creation, recommended checking spam and waiting a few minutes, then re-triggered the message from the admin tool which resolved the issue.

case_summary: Customer requested help because payment succeeded and there was concern the missing confirmation email meant the order failed.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the purchase was valid and explained the confirmation email had been delayed. The message was re-sent and no refund was needed.
customer_message: I was charged but never received the confirmation email, so I'm not sure whether the order went through.

case_summary: Order was placed and charged correctly, but the customer did not receive the confirmation email or receipt.
resolution_text: Support checked the notification queue, confirmed a backlog delay, and re-sent the receipt. Customer confirmed receipt after checking spam.
customer_message: My order was charged correctly, but I didn't receive the receipt or confirmation email.

case_summary: Customer worried payment may have failed because the inbox never received a confirmation after checkout.
resolution_text: Agent verified the charge and order ID, explained that email delivery can lag, and reissued the confirmation email successfully.
customer_message: I finished checkout and there's still no confirmation in my inbox.

case_summary: Customer completed a purchase during a notification outage and never got the post-purchase confirmation email.
resolution_text: Escalation matched the charge to a valid order, confirmed the outage window, and manually re-sent the email while advising the customer to whitelist the sender.

case_summary: Caller received a bank charge but no checkout confirmation email, leading to uncertainty about the order status.
resolution_text: Support validated the order in the backend, confirmed the email address, and sent a new confirmation email after ruling out payment failure.

case_summary: Customer cannot reset the password because the reset link expired before it was used.
resolution_text: Agent generated a fresh reset link, advised the customer to use the newest email only, and confirmed the password reset completed successfully.
customer_message: The reset link expires before I can use it, so I still can't get into my account.

case_summary: Password reset email never arrived, so the customer could not regain account access.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the account email, asked the customer to check spam, and re-sent the password reset email. The second email arrived and the issue was resolved.
customer_message: I'm not receiving the password reset email, so I can't log in.

case_summary: User reports that every password reset link says invalid or expired immediately after opening it.
resolution_text: Escalation found multiple reset requests invalidating earlier links. Customer was told to use only the latest message and a fresh link fixed the problem.

case_summary: Caller could not sign in because the reset email had not shown up and prior links no longer worked.
resolution_text: Agent checked deliverability, verified the account address, issued one final reset email, and instructed the customer to ignore older reset messages. Login access was restored.

case_summary: Customer asked for cancellation help after being locked out when password reset emails were missing.
resolution_text: Support re-sent the reset email, confirmed inbox delivery after spam check, and the customer regained access so the cancellation request was withdrawn.
customer_message: I need help because I'm locked out and the password reset emails never arrive.

case_summary: Reset email received late, and the link had already expired by the time the customer opened it.
resolution_text: Agent explained reset links are single-use and time-limited, sent a new one, and confirmed the customer successfully changed the password.
customer_message: The reset email showed up too late and the link was already expired.

case_summary: Customer clicked a password reset link from an earlier email and kept seeing an invalid-token message.
resolution_text: Support clarified that multiple reset requests deactivate older links, sent a fresh reset email, and the customer completed the flow using the newest link.
customer_message: Every reset link I open says it's invalid.

case_summary: Customer was stuck in the reset flow because reset emails were delayed and older links had expired.
resolution_text: Escalation reviewed email logs, re-issued the reset email, and recommended waiting for the latest message only. The customer reset the password successfully.

case_summary: Customer sees two card charges for a single checkout attempt and believes they were billed twice.
resolution_text: Support verified only one completed order existed. The second charge was a temporary authorization hold that would drop automatically within a few business days.
customer_message: I only placed one order, but my card shows two charges.

case_summary: Two pending transactions appeared after one purchase, but the customer only intended to submit the order once.
resolution_text: Agent confirmed a single successful payment capture and explained that the second pending line was a duplicate authorization hold, not an extra settled charge.
customer_message: I checked out once and now I see two pending charges.

case_summary: Customer was charged twice after retrying checkout, but only one order record was created.
resolution_text: Escalation found one settled payment and one voided authorization. Customer was informed the bank would release the duplicate hold automatically.

case_summary: Caller reported two bank notifications for the same order and feared double billing.
resolution_text: Agent reviewed payment status, confirmed one capture and one pending authorization, and advised the customer that the duplicate hold would disappear without further action.

case_summary: Customer requested a refund because they thought the order was billed twice.
resolution_text: Support confirmed only one completed charge and one temporary authorization. Customer withdrew the refund request after the explanation.
customer_message: I think I was billed twice for the same order and want the extra charge refunded.

case_summary: Customer clicked Pay twice after a slow checkout page and now sees two transactions.
resolution_text: Support checked the gateway logs, verified a single order and a single settled payment, and explained the other entry was an authorization hold caused by the retry.
customer_message: The checkout page was slow, I clicked pay again, and now I have two transactions.

case_summary: Customer's statement shows a second pending charge from the same merchant right after purchase.
resolution_text: Agent matched both payment IDs, confirmed one payment was reversed automatically, and explained bank statements can temporarily show both lines.
customer_message: My statement is showing a second pending charge right after the purchase.

case_summary: Customer insisted there were two completed charges for one order, requiring deeper payment review.
resolution_text: Escalation traced the payment events and confirmed one capture plus one canceled authorization. Documentation from the gateway was provided to reassure the customer.

case_summary: Caller said checkout froze, so they retried and later noticed two identical card charges.
resolution_text: Support confirmed there was no duplicate order and only one final settlement. The extra line item was identified as a temporary hold that would expire.

case_summary: Customer says a valid promo code is rejected at checkout even though the offer is still active.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the code was valid but limited to first-time purchases. The customer was given the correct eligibility details and applied a different eligible offer successfully.
customer_message: My promo code is still active, but checkout keeps saying it's invalid.

case_summary: Promo code from a campaign email returns invalid during checkout.
resolution_text: Agent reviewed the campaign rules, found the code required a minimum cart value, and explained the requirement. Customer adjusted the cart and applied the code successfully.
customer_message: The discount code from your campaign email won't work at checkout.

case_summary: Customer reports a marketing coupon should work but the checkout page keeps rejecting it.
resolution_text: Escalation found the code was region-limited and not applicable to the customer's account locale. Support clarified the restriction and offered an alternative active promotion.

case_summary: Caller tried to redeem a discount code but the system said the code was invalid.
resolution_text: Agent verified the code was active, then found it applied only to annual plans and not monthly subscriptions. Customer switched plan type and redeemed the discount.

case_summary: Customer requested a refund after paying full price because a promo code was rejected during purchase.
resolution_text: Support reviewed the code rules, confirmed the customer used an ineligible code, and applied a post-purchase courtesy credit instead of a full refund.
customer_message: My promo code was rejected, so I ended up paying full price and want help.

case_summary: Discount code looked active but failed during checkout on an account that had used a similar offer before.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the campaign was one-time-use per account. Customer was informed of the usage limit and the checkout completed without further errors.
customer_message: The code looks valid, but it fails when I try to apply it during checkout.

case_summary: Customer entered a promo code copied from social media and got an expired or invalid error.
resolution_text: Agent validated the code and found the public post referenced an expired campaign. A currently active code was shared and the customer completed checkout.
customer_message: I copied the promo code and it says expired or invalid.

case_summary: Promo code kept failing even though the customer believed all campaign conditions were met.
resolution_text: Escalation found the offer excluded gift-card items in the basket. After the excluded item was removed, the code applied normally.

case_summary: Customer enters MFA code successfully but is sent back to the login page instead of getting into the account.
resolution_text: Support identified a stale browser session cookie, asked the customer to clear cookies and sign in again, which resolved the MFA login loop.
customer_message: I enter the MFA code successfully, but the app sends me back to the login page.

case_summary: After completing two-factor verification in the mobile app, the customer returns to the sign-in screen repeatedly.
resolution_text: Agent had the customer update the app and clear local session data. After signing in again, the MFA loop stopped.
customer_message: After two-factor verification, it keeps taking me back to sign-in.

case_summary: User passes multi-factor authentication but never reaches the dashboard because the session refresh fails.
resolution_text: Escalation traced the issue to an outdated app build with a token refresh bug. Updating the client resolved the post-MFA redirect loop.

case_summary: Caller can enter the verification code but is immediately asked to log in again.
resolution_text: Agent walked the customer through clearing cached browser data and disabling an old bookmarked login path. The next login worked normally.

case_summary: Customer asked to cancel because they were stuck in a login loop even after passing MFA.
resolution_text: Support found corrupted session data, had the customer sign out everywhere and retry on the latest app version, and access was restored.
customer_message: I can't get into my account because it loops back to login after MFA.

case_summary: The app accepts the one-time passcode but instantly returns to the authentication screen.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the account was healthy and resolved the issue by having the customer force-close the app, clear local storage, and re-authenticate.
customer_message: The one-time passcode is accepted, then I'm returned to the authentication screen.

case_summary: Customer completes verification but the website keeps bouncing back to sign-in.
resolution_text: Agent identified blocked third-party cookies in the browser, recommended enabling required cookies for the session, and the user logged in successfully.
customer_message: Verification works, but the site keeps bouncing me back to the login page.

case_summary: Repeated MFA success still led to a login loop across multiple attempts.
resolution_text: Escalation confirmed the customer was using an outdated deep-link path from an old email. Access worked after navigating to the current login page and starting a fresh session.

case_summary: Customer received notice that the refund was processed but the bank balance has not updated yet.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the refund had been issued and explained that banks can take several business days to post the credit to the original payment method.
customer_message: I got a message saying my refund was processed, but the money isn't back in my account yet.

case_summary: Refund email says completed, but the card statement still does not show the returned amount.
resolution_text: Agent verified the refund transaction ID and explained that card issuers often display the credit after additional processing time.
customer_message: The refund email says completed, but I still don't see anything on my card statement.

case_summary: Customer insists the refund was never sent because the bank account still looks unchanged.
resolution_text: Escalation confirmed the refund settled from the merchant side and provided the refund reference while explaining bank posting delays.

case_summary: Caller says support promised a refund, but nothing has appeared on the debit card yet.
resolution_text: Agent confirmed the refund date, noted that debit-card refunds can take multiple business days, and advised the customer when to follow up if still missing.

case_summary: Customer submitted a second refund request because the first processed refund was not visible in banking yet.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the original refund was already completed and explained expected bank posting windows, so no duplicate refund was issued.
customer_message: My refund shows as processed on your side, but my bank still hasn't posted it.

case_summary: Subscription cancellation refund shows as processed in the account but not on the customer's card.
resolution_text: Support verified the refund status and explained that issuer processing time can vary by bank, usually several business days.
customer_message: My account says the refund was processed, but nothing has appeared on my card.

case_summary: Customer wants to know why the refund appears as done on our side but pending nowhere on the bank side.
resolution_text: Agent shared the refund timestamp and explained that some banks do not show a pending credit before final posting.
customer_message: Why does the refund show as done in the app when my bank doesn't show it at all?

case_summary: Customer expected same-day refund visibility after cancellation but the account still showed no credit.
resolution_text: Escalation confirmed the refund left the merchant processor and asked the customer to allow the standard bank settlement period before escalating further.

case_summary: Caller says the app shows refunded, but the original payment method has not been credited yet.
resolution_text: Support confirmed the refund completed internally and advised waiting through the bank's posting timeframe, then contacting support with the reference number if needed.

case_summary: Customer says the subscription was canceled but a renewal charge still appeared this billing cycle.
resolution_text: Support found the cancellation was scheduled for period end, so the renewal processed before access ended. The timing was explained and a courtesy refund was issued where policy allowed.
customer_message: I canceled my subscription, but I was still charged for renewal.

case_summary: Customer believed they had canceled already, but a new subscription charge was posted.
resolution_text: Agent checked the account and saw auto-renew remained active because cancellation was not completed in the final confirmation step. Support canceled the plan and handled the billing outcome according to policy.
customer_message: I thought I had already canceled, so why did another subscription charge go through?

case_summary: Customer disputes a renewal fee after attempting to cancel from the mobile app.
resolution_text: Escalation found the in-app cancellation did not finish due to store-side interruption. Customer was guided through the correct cancellation path and the disputed renewal was resolved.

case_summary: Caller says the subscription showed canceled in their mind, but they were billed again this month.
resolution_text: Agent reviewed the timeline and confirmed the subscription stayed active until the end of the paid term. The customer misunderstood the delayed cancellation model, which was clarified.

case_summary: Customer requested a refund after being charged following what they thought was a completed cancellation.
resolution_text: Support verified the cancellation attempt was incomplete, canceled the subscription immediately, and issued a refund under the exception policy.
customer_message: I believed my subscription was canceled, but I was charged again and want a refund.

case_summary: Customer turned off the service and expected no further charges, but renewal still happened.
resolution_text: Support explained that disabling usage does not cancel billing. The subscription itself was still active, so the customer was shown the correct cancellation workflow.
customer_message: I turned the service off and expected no more charges, but renewal still happened.

case_summary: Customer canceled on the last day and was still charged overnight.
resolution_text: Agent explained that the renewal had already queued before cancellation completed. Support applied the appropriate policy outcome and confirmed the plan was no longer renewing.
customer_message: I canceled on the last day and still got charged overnight.

case_summary: Customer says they removed the card but still got charged for renewal.
resolution_text: Escalation confirmed that removing a payment method does not cancel the subscription contract. The account was canceled properly and the charge dispute was resolved.

case_summary: Caller thought uninstalling the app canceled the subscription, but another monthly charge appeared.
resolution_text: Agent clarified that uninstalling does not end billing, verified the active plan, and walked the customer through cancellation. Billing remediation followed policy.
